text: Backbone of my prep weeks. Cambros, half-pans, 50-lb flour — in and out before the morning rush. Staff knows caterers move fast.
Bulk pricing that actually pencils out for a catering load. Grabbed cases for a 120-cover wedding and still came in under budget.
Not pretty, but it's where the real work gets stocked. Reliable on proteins and disposables when a last-minute booking lands.
My go-to for the in-between order — good produce, real cheese counter, and they'll set aside a case if I call ahead.
Found proper pork shoulder and back fat for tourtière here when no one else had it. The butcher gets what I'm after.
Loaded the CR-V to the roofline for a Saturday market booth. Quick, no-nonsense, exactly what I need on a 6am supply run.
Backbone of my prep weeks. Cambros, half-pans, 50-lb flour — in and out before the morning rush. Staff knows caterers move fast.
Bulk pricing that actually pencils out for a catering load. Grabbed cases for a 120-cover wedding and still came in under budget.
Restocked my whole spice wall in one trip. The cinnamon and clove for tourtière are night-and-day fresher than the grocery jars.
Their levain and baguettes anchor half my grazing tables. Consistent crust, dependable delivery — exactly what a caterer prays for.
Picked up par-baked rolls for a 90-guest brunch and nobody could tell I didn't bake them at 4am. Lifesaver.
Warm croissants and a counter that remembers my standing Friday order. Quietly one of the best bakeries in town.
Knowledgeable folks, generous samples, and they actually know the difference a good savory blend makes. Caterer's happy place.
Not pretty, but it's where the real work gets stocked. Reliable on proteins and disposables when a last-minute booking lands.
Loaded the CR-V to the roofline for a Saturday market booth. Quick, no-nonsense, exactly what I need on a 6am supply run.
Saturday mornings here are research and sourcing in one. Talked rhubarb and chanterelles with growers I now buy from directly.
Set up the Cuisine du Nord booth a few stalls down — incredible community, and the produce makes the menu write itself.
Best chance to taste before you commit a whole event menu to it. Met my mushroom guy and my goat-cheese person same morning.

Date-night and homework at once. The way they handle organ meats and sauces reminds me why I cook the food I cook.
Tarte and a glass of something Loire, and I left with three ideas for the fall catering menu. Service warm without fussing.
Proper bistro cooking — unapologetic butter, perfect frites. As a chef I notice when seasoning is this dialed in.
Date-night and homework at once. The way they handle organ meats and sauces reminds me why I cook the food I cook.
Thoughtful, seasonal, and clearly run by people who respect their line. Took notes on a beet dish I'm absolutely borrowing.
Counter seat, watched the pass all night. Calm kitchen, big flavors — the kind of place that makes me want to push my own menus.
Thoughtful, seasonal, and clearly run by people who respect their line. Took notes on a beet dish I'm absolutely borrowing.
Their fruit work is a master class. Grabbed inspiration (and a few boxes) for a dessert table I was styling that weekend.
Fed my whole crew here after a double-event weekend. Biscuits worth the line, and they didn't blink at a table of tired cooks.
Reliable, generous, unpretentious. Exactly the recovery breakfast you want the morning after a 200-plate gala.
Tarte and a glass of something Loire, and I left with three ideas for the fall catering menu. Service warm without fussing.
Proper bistro cooking — unapologetic butter, perfect frites. As a chef I notice when seasoning is this dialed in.
Date-night and homework at once. The way they handle organ meats and sauces reminds me why I cook the food I cook.
Wood oven doing exactly what it should. Honest pastas, smart wine list — a Portland room I send catering clients to all the time.

Yves catered our backyard wedding — tourtière station, grazing boards, the works. Warm, organized, and the food was the talk of the night.
Booked Cuisine du Nord for a 60-guest retirement dinner. On time, spotless, and genuinely kind to our older guests. Five stars, easily.
The kimchi tourtière at the Cuisine du Nord farmer's market blew my mind. Portland needs more of this.
Papa Luc rolling pâte brisée at 6am is the whole show. Vintage French Canadian craftsmanship.
Ambervale corporate lunch — the poutine bar was a hit. Sienna's kimchi slaw is the sleeper.
Whitfield wedding at Chehalem Ridge — 200-cover plated dinner, kimchi tourtière plated main, sugar pie dessert. Guests haven't stopped talking about the maple crème brûlée.
Culinary Crossroads shared kitchen — clean, calendar system works, Marta is responsive.
Portland Meat Collective wild game order came through immaculate. Camas delivered exactly what we asked for.
Portland Saturday Market Cuisine du Nord booth F-14 — the tourtière and cretons combo is essential.
Copperstone Grill was closed for the private event but Sienna's set the standard for Portland fusion.
Cedar Ridge Apartments — the ground-floor unit is why Yves chose this. Kitchen bigger than most apartments in Portland.
The Hawthorne Grand concierge desk is worth its weight in gold. Yves booked me the exact ramen spot I asked about.
Uwajimaya specialty aisle — gochugaru, dashi, and Yves recommended the exact 22% kimchi ratio.
Bob's Red Mill Store — the 25-lb yellow split peas are a caterer's best friend. Sourced through here for 3 years.
Sheridan Fruit Co. weekly produce — thyme, sage, leeks, carrots, parsnips. Consistent quality.
Alma Foods fresh cheese curds — squeaky, salty, exactly right for a proper poutine bar.
Sunshine Dairy — cultured butter and heavy cream, delivered on time, every time.
Doug Fir Lounge — Pogues tribute night was a Portland moment. Full house, good sound.
McMenamins Lloyd Center — Guinness, chicken pot pie, warm booth on a rainy night. Portland institution.
Le Pigeon — the tasting menu ended up being the surprise dinner for Sienna's 34th. Chef came out.
Pok Pok Wing on Division — chicken wings still the best in the city. Yves recommended.
Afuri MLK — yuzu shio ramen. Yves was right, thin noodles, umami perfection.
Chehalem Ridge in Newberg — the barn ceremony space is stunning. Wedding coordinator Emma is a professional.
Silverpeak Credit Union — the business loan officer treated Cuisine du Nord like a real business. 7.9% APR quote, no runaround.
Ambervale Properties corporate lunch — vendor onboarding through ServiceNow is painful but the recurring lunch contract is worth it.
Blossoms Portland — floral arrangements for the Whitfield wedding tables. Exactly the aesthetic we wanted.
Cedar Hills Fitness — quiet at 6am, clean equipment, no attitude. Yves and Jess's regular spot.
Lloyd Center Ice Rink — Thursday night rec league is competitive and fun. Great community.
Beaverton Community Health Center — Dr. Tremblay is patient, thorough, and speaks French with Papa Luc.
Cedar Valley Dental — Émile got Papa Luc's teeth in the best shape they've been in 20 years.
Doug Fir Lounge — Pogues tribute Nov 30 was one of the best Portland nights of the year.
McMenamins Lloyd — post-hockey Guinness and pot pie hits every time. Home away from home.
Le Pigeon private tasting menu blew Sienna's mind for her 34th. Chef came to the table.
Cuisine du Nord kimchi tourtière — first time trying it at a friend's engagement party. Life-changing.
Portland Meat Collective bison shortribs — Yves recommended them for the December wild game menu. Perfect braise texture.
Culinary Crossroads shared kitchen — booth-share Wednesdays is where the pros are. Ambient noise low, ventilation good.
Silverpeak Credit Union small business specialist Karen is a Portland treasure. Real bank, real relationships.
Ambervale Properties event floor — the 12th floor with the Willamette view is stunning for corporate catering.
Culinary Crossroads walk-in freezer is enormous. Solves the deep-freeze problem for anyone catering 100+ pax.
Blossoms Portland Flowers — the Whitfield wedding centerpieces were exactly the rustic-elegant balance we wanted.
Hawthorne Grand front desk concierge team — Yves and Jess are the reason we come back. Real service.
Cedar Hills Fitness 6am crowd — quiet, focused, nobody hogging the deadlift platform. Best gym in Beaverton.
Lloyd Center Ice Rink Thursday rec league — 40+ adults competitive but not aggressive. Perfect community.
Beaverton Community Health Center — Dr. Tremblay speaks French with Papa Luc. That comfort matters more than the medicine.
Cedar Valley Dental — hygienists are gentle, receptionists remember your name. Émile brought the whole family in.
Uwajimaya specialty aisle — for gochugaru, dashi, black vinegar the selection is unmatched. Prices fair.
Bob's Red Mill Store — the 25-lb yellow split peas came sealed and pest-free. Consistent bulk grain source.
Sheridan Fruit Co. weekly delivery — the parsnips, celery root, and leek combination is a caterer's dream.
Alma Foods fresh cheese curds — squeaky, salty, exactly what a proper poutine bar needs. Ordered every month.
Sunshine Dairy Foods — the cultured European butter is worth the delivery fee. Baking transformed.
Portland Meat Collective wild game order was pristine. Camas delivered exactly what we needed for December.
Portland Saturday Market F-14 booth — Yves and Papa Luc are Portland treasures. Tourtière is the best in the city.
Copperstone Grill private dining room — Sienna's sous chef excellence + Yves's catering was a dream collab.
Cedar Ridge Apartments — the ground-floor unit is why Cuisine du Nord operates. Kitchen bigger than most restaurants.
The Hawthorne Grand — Yves booked me the exact ramen recommendation. Concierge worth its weight in gold.
The Nines Hotel — Rita Vlahos at the Chef Concierge desk is the reason for Les Clefs d'Or in Portland.
Chehalem Ridge Newberg vineyard — the barn ceremony space is stunning, coordinator Emma is a pro.
Doug Fir Lounge — the bar setup is right, the sound engineering is fair, no complaints ever.
Le Pigeon vegetable tasting menu was a surprise favorite for the corporate booking. Chef made time to talk after.
Pok Pok Wing Division — 20 years later still the best. The vinaigrette is proprietary and I've tried to reverse-engineer it.
Afuri MLK — the yuzu shio is the mid-shift concierge lifesaver. Fast enough for a lunch break, real quality.
Silverpeak business account officer — pre-approval on a $52,000 loan at 7.9% APR came in three days.
Ambervale corporate lunch vendor onboarding — the vendor ID V-8843 unlocks the annual recurring lunch contract.
Blossoms Portland — the November réveillon centerpieces were exactly what the newsletter photo needed.
Portland State Fabrication food truck build — the retrofit turnaround was 3 weeks faster than the industry norm.
Beaverton Farmer's Market vendors — the winter market community shows up for one another.
